verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I membrane protein of the p24 trafficking family with a single transmembrane domain. NCBI explicitly states it localizes to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae, establishing a documented dual localization. The protein cycles between these compartments as part of vesicular protein trafficking and heteromeric secretase complex function. While its canonical role centers on Golgi/trafficking function, the documented PM pool qualifies it as contextual rather than endomembrane-resident-only.
